Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 1. Understand the Goal: The objective is to analyze a provided event log of customer support ticket resolutions. I need to identify cases with long resolution times, find root causes (escalations, wait times, etc.), and propose insights/recommendations.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Data: I need to go through each Case ID and calculate the total duration from Receive Ticket to Close Ticket. Case 101: Start: 2024-03-01 08:00 End: 2024-03-01 10:15 Duration: 2 hours 15 minutes. Path: Receive -> Triage -> Assign -> Investigate -> Resolve -> Close. Escalations: None. Wait times: ... Observation: Very fast, standard flow. Case 102: ... Observation: Long duration. Major delay is between Investigate Issue (Level 2) and Resolve Ticket. ... Case 103: ... Case 104: ... Case 105: ...

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Synthesize Findings (Drafting the Response): Part 1: Identify Long Cases. Case 101: ~2.25 hrs. Case 102: ~25.17 hrs. Case 103: ~1.33 hrs. Case 104: ~24.17 hrs. Case 105: ~57.42 hrs. Conclusion: Cases 102, 104, and 105 are outliers compared to 101 and 103. Part 2: Root Causes. Escalations: Case 102 and 105 involve escalation. Case 104 does not but still took long. Wait Times (The Overnight Gap): ... Observation: All long cases have a gap crossing from one calendar day to the next ... Part 3: Insights & Recommendations. Bottleneck: The transition from Investigate to Resolve is the main delay point.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: 4. Refining the Analysis (Self-Correction/Detail Check): Calendar Check: March 1, 2024 is indeed a Friday. March 2 is Saturday. March 3 is Sunday. This explains the Overnight gaps perfectly. ... Correction: March 3, 2024 is a Sunday. ... Actually, looking at Case 105: Escalate: Mar 1 (Fri) 14:00. Investigate (L2): Mar 2 (Sat) 14:00. (24 hours wait). ... Re-evaluating the Root Cause: Its not just Weekend. Its specifically the gap between Investigation and Resolution when the ticket moves past a certain time in the day ... Pattern: Any ticket where Investigate Issue happens after ~13:00/14:00 on Friday seems to wait until the next morning ... Key Insight: The primary bottleneck is the Handover/Shift Boundary.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Refined Root Cause: 1. Shift/Day Boundary: Late-day investigations wait until next morning to resolve. 2. Escalation Handover: Escalated tickets wait significantly longer for Level 2 to pick them up (24+ hours in Case 105). 3. Level 2 Availability: Level 2 seems to have slower intake or specific hours compared to Level 1.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 5. Structuring the Output: Section 1: Duration Analysis. Calculate and list durations. Highlight outliers. Section 2: Root Cause Analysis. Break down the specific delays (Escalation vs. Waiting). Note the time-of-day impact. Section 3: Insights & Recommendations. Actionable advice based on the findings (SLA management, Shift handovers, Escalation protocols).

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 6. Final Review: Does this answer the prompt? Yes. Is the logic sound? Yes. Is the formatting clear? Yes. (Self-correction: Ensure I explicitly mention the calendar context as it adds value to the weekend observation, even if the root cause is shift-based). ... Okay, ready to write.
